evals.prompt: Write requirements for a real-time notification system with WebSocket support, email fallback, and user preferences.
evals.expected_output: A complete requirements.md covering problem, personas, journeys, features, and acceptance criteria
expectations: Invoked brainstorm skill before template filling
Wrote output to requirements.md in the spec directory
Followed template.md structure exactly — all sections present
No [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers remain in completed document
Defined at least 2 distinct user personas with different goals
User personas are MECE — no two personas overlap in role or motivation
Defined user journeys for each persona
User journeys cover both happy path and error/recovery paths
Feature requirements use MoSCoW prioritization (Must/Should/Could/Won't)
Feature requirements are MECE — no two stories describe the same capability
Each feature has acceptance criteria
Acceptance criteria are MECE — no two criteria test the same condition
Document contains NO technical implementation details (no code, architecture, or database design)
Document contains NO API specifications
Waited for user confirmation between sections
Ran validation checklist before declaring complete

evals.prompt: Write requirements for a CLI tool that has no UI — a batch data migration script.
evals.expected_output: Requirements adapted for non-UI context — no user journeys through a GUI, focus on inputs/outputs/error handling
expectations: Personas reflect actual users (DevOps engineer, DBA) not generic end-users
Journeys describe CLI invocation patterns, not UI flows
Acceptance criteria are testable via command-line output, exit codes, and log files
No UI-specific language (no 'the user sees', 'the page displays')
Features address error handling, idempotency, and recovery

evals.prompt: Write requirements, but the user provides very little context — just 'build a todo app'.
evals.expected_output: Skill probes for clarification before writing, does not assume scope
expectations: Invoked brainstorm to explore the user's intent before template filling
Asked clarifying questions about target users, scale, and key features
Did NOT fill in requirements with generic assumptions without user input
Waited for user responses before proceeding to documentation

evals.prompt: Continue requirements for spec 002 which has a partially completed requirements.md with 3 [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers.
evals.expected_output: Resume from existing state, address clarification markers
expectations: Read existing requirements.md from the spec directory
Identified the 3 [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers
Addressed each marker through discovery and user interaction
Did NOT rewrite sections that were already complete
Replaced all markers with actual content
